title: Mirpur Jain Temple
tagline: Mirpur Jain Temple — spiritual town of Rajasthan

overview.short: Mirpur Jain Temple (Aati Prachin Hamirpura Bhidbhanjan Parshvanath) is a site of Śvetāmbara Jain pilgrimage situated in Mirpur, a fortified village in the Sirohi district of Rajast
overview.description: Mirpur Jain Temple (Aati Prachin Hamirpura Bhidbhanjan Parshvanath) is a site of Śvetāmbara Jain pilgrimage situated in Mirpur, a fortified village in the Sirohi district of Rajasthan, India. This Temple is Dedicated to the 23rd Tirthankara, Lord Parshvanath (Bhidbhanjan Parshvanath). The village has four Jain temples.

topPlaces.name: Mirpur, Sirohi
topPlaces.description: Mirpur is a village located in the Sirohi District of Rajasthan, India. The village is also known for its Jain tirtha called Mirpur Jain Temple and Mirpur Jahaj Temple.

topPlaces.name: Sirohi
topPlaces.description: Sirohi is a town, located in Sirohi district in southern Rajasthan state in western India. It is the administrative headquarters of Sirohi District and was formerly the capital of the princely state of Sirohi ruled by Deora Chauhan Rajput rulers.

topPlaces.name: Sirohi State
topPlaces.description: Kingdom of Sirohi, known as Sirohi State in colonial times, was a kingdom and later princely state in present-day Rajasthan state of India. The state was founded in 1311 CE by the Deora subclan of Chauhan Rajputs and lasted for six centuries till 1949 CE. The Sirohi sword is a traditional Indian weapon originating from the town of Sirohi.

items.note: Mirpur is a village located in the Sirohi District of Rajasthan, India. The village is also known for its Jain tirtha called Mirpur Jain Temple and Mirpur Jahaj Temple.

Sirohi is a town, located in Sirohi district in southern Rajasthan state in western India. It is the administrative headquarters of Sirohi District and was formerly the capital of the princely state of Sirohi ruled by Deora Chauhan Rajput rulers.

Kingdom of Sirohi, known as Sirohi State in colonial times, was a kingdom and later princely state in present-day Rajasthan state of India. The state was founded in 1311 CE by the Deora subclan of Chauhan Rajputs and lasted for six centuries till 1949 CE. The Sirohi sword is a traditional Indian weapon originating from the town of Sirohi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mirpur Jain Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mirpur Jain Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mirpur Jain Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (113 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (94 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mirpur Jain Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mirpur Jain Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mirpur Jain Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mirpur Jain Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mirpur Jain Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mirpur, Sirohi, Sirohi, Sirohi State.
